arts.ttl: Werder Bremen vs Bayern Munich: Gamethread and Live Blog
arts.sum: Bayern Munich head up north to face SV Werder Bremen, coming fresh of the back of a record-breaking 9-2 in the Champions League during midweek. While you’re waiting for the game, why not check out our preview podcast?

arts.ttl: Elfmeister: Harry Kane’s spotless penalty record for Bayern Munich and England
arts.sum: The 31-year-old is so lethal from the spot that he’s the best penalty taker among the continent’s top scorers, converting every single one he has taken since last year: Since the beginning of 2023, Harry Kane has converted 21 of 21 penalties for club and country (13 of 13 for Bayern).

arts.ttl: Max Eberl boldly claims that all teams can defend well…even Bayern Munich?
arts.sum: You might want to look at the ratings your defenders got, Max “Today, all teams can defend well, that’s the easiest thing to do.

arts.ttl: The world’s worst restart: Naby Keïta’s disastrous one-year spell at Werder Bremen
arts.sum: With Naby Keita set for a familiar spell on the sidelines, here’s a recap of the events surrounding one of the Bundesliga’s worst ever signings.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich coach Vincent Kompany elaborates on how he wants his players to defend
arts.sum: When you play against Bayern, you rarely have the ball and have to work hard to win it back.” Bayern Munich signed some players in the summer but came away with a bit of misfortune on the defensive side of things.

arts.ttl: Saturday's Bundesliga predictions including Werder Bremen vs. Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's Bundesliga fixtures, including Werder Bremen vs. Bayern Munich.

arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Manchester United, West Ham eyeing Bayern Munich’s Leon Goretzka; Bayern scouting Mason Greenwood (!?); Arsenal, Liverpool, Newcastle want Leroy Sané; Thomas Müller takes a tumble; and MORE!
arts.sum: Manchester United and West Ham are among the clubs interested in Bayern Munich’s Leon Goretzka, who is ‘upset’ over his lack of game time with the German giants. The Sun report that both Manchester United and West Ham have both already shown interest in the Germany international, while other Premier League sides could well make a move if a Bayern Munich exit appears nailed on.

arts.ttl: Aleksandar Pavlović reviews Bayern Munich’s season so far
arts.sum: We need to keep complete control,” said Pavlović about the squads performance against the two German clubs (captured by @iMiaSanMia ). In a recent interview, Pavlović spoke in particular about Bayern’s first two Bundesliga games, against VFL Wolfsburg and SC Freiburg.

arts.ttl: Report: Leon Goretzka linked to big club for potential January transfer move
arts.sum: In a recent interview (via @caughtoffside ), reporter Christian Falk said, “Manchester United were previously interested in [Goretzka’s] services and they’ve seen that they can make good deals with Bayern Munich having landed both Matthijs de Ligt and Noussair Mazraoui in the summer. Matthijs de Ligt and Noussair Mazraoui each made a successful transfer to Manchester United while Leon Goretzka and Kingsley Coman were unable to complete a deal.

arts.ttl: Vincent Kompany points to past journey from refugee to Premier League great to explain how he could believe in his own ability to coach Bayern Munich
arts.sum: But, in a simple yet eloquent monologue about believing in one’s ability to succeed, Vincent Kompany demonstrates that he does not need the crutch of a good result or multiple accolades to help justify his competence for the Bayern job. The shock when Vincent Kompany moved from a relegated Burnley team to Bayern Munich was worldwide, with many believing that the young manager would not have the requisite ability to succeed in the job.
